definition: Top-10 S&P 500 weight at 35-38% (vs 27% at 2000 peak) creates mechanical amplification risk through coupling of passive (~55% AUM), momentum (70%+ overlap with AI names), and systematic strategies, with PC1 explaining 45-50% of return variance vs 30-35% historical.
evidence: Top-10 S&P 500 weight ~35-38% vs 27% at 2000 peak
Momentum factor 70%+ overlap with AI names
PC1 explains 45-50% of return variance vs 30-35% historical
Momentum ENB collapsed to ~25-35, below 2007 quant quake (~30-40) and March 2020 (~40-50)
Momentum-quality inter-factor correlation tripled from +0.15-0.25 to +0.50-0.60
open_questions: Whether $40-60B/month international capital flows create a non-linearity mechanism absent in prior concentration episodes
What would trigger rebalancing cascades vs gradual reversion
Whether 3 years of post-ChatGPT data is sufficient for definitive factor attribution
